name: AWS MCP Servers Samples (Official)
what_it_does: Official AWS sample MCP servers from aws-samples organization demonstrating how to build MCP servers for AWS services — including samples for Amazon Bedrock, EC2, S3, IAM, CloudWatch, and other AWS services, providing reference implementations for AWS-native AI agent development and showing AWS service integration patterns for MCP.
use_cases: Using AWS Bedrock models from AI agent workflows via sample patterns
Accessing EC2 instance metadata from infrastructure management agents
Integrating CloudWatch metrics and alarms from monitoring agents
Using IAM roles and policies from security management agents
Accessing S3 data via pattern-based MCP implementation from data agents
Learning how to build AWS-integrated MCP servers from development agents
not_for: Teams needing production-ready implementations (samples are reference/learning code)
Teams using Azure or GCP (AWS-specific patterns)
Teams needing deep single-service coverage (use dedicated service MCPs)
best_when: A developer or agent needs AWS service integration patterns and reference code — for building custom AWS MCP servers, learning AWS+MCP architecture, or accessing multiple AWS services.
avoid_when: You need production-ready, fully-featured AWS MCP servers — use dedicated official or community AWS MCPs. These are samples/references, not production tools.

auth.notes: AWS IAM credentials via standard AWS credential chain. Each sample may require different IAM permissions. IAM role recommended. Environment variables, AWS credentials file, or instance metadata.

pricing.free_tier_limits: AWS free tier varies by service (S3, EC2, Lambda, etc.)
pricing.notes: Sample code is open source and free. AWS service usage may incur costs. AWS account with billing required. Bedrock model access requires separate enablement.

known_agent_gotchas: These are SAMPLES — not production-ready, may have missing error handling
Each sample targets different AWS services — check which sample covers your use case
Bedrock samples require model access enablement in AWS console (not automatic)
IAM permissions vary per sample — review required permissions before deployment
Samples may use different Python/Node.js versions — check compatibility
Official aws-samples — good reference quality but explicitly not for production as-is
agent_readiness.security_notes: TLS via AWS SDK. IAM with least-privilege per sample. SOC2, HIPAA, GDPR, FedRAMP. Official aws-samples. Use IAM roles over static keys. Review IAM policies needed for each sample before deployment.

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS via AWS SDK. IAM credentials. SOC2, HIPAA, GDPR, FedRAMP. Official aws-samples. Use IAM roles. Review IAM requirements per sample.
